indemnify.definition: To compensate someone for a loss or damage they suffered
indemnify.plain_english: You agree to pay for any losses the other party suffers
indemnify.risk_note: One-sided indemnification means only YOU pay

indemnification.definition: A contractual obligation to compensate another party for losses
indemnification.plain_english: A promise to cover the other party's losses or legal costs

liability.definition: Legal responsibility for something, especially financial obligations
liability.plain_english: Who is responsible if something goes wrong
liability.risk_note: Look for caps on liability — unlimited liability is very risky

limitation of liability.definition: A clause that caps the maximum amount one party can be held responsible for
limitation of liability.plain_english: There's a maximum amount you'd have to pay if things go wrong
limitation of liability.risk_note: Make sure the cap protects you, not just them

non-compete.definition: A clause preventing a party from engaging in competing business activities
non-compete.plain_english: You can't work for or start a competing business for a period after this contract ends

non-solicitation.definition: Prevents recruiting employees or clients of the other party
non-solicitation.plain_english: You can't try to hire their employees or steal their customers

non-disparagement.definition: Prevents making negative public statements about each other
non-disparagement.plain_english: You can't publicly say bad things about the other party
non-disparagement.risk_note: Can limit your ability to leave honest reviews

termination for convenience.definition: The right to end a contract at any time without needing a reason
termination for convenience.plain_english: They can cancel whenever they want, no reason needed
termination for convenience.risk_note: Very risky if only one party has this right

force majeure.definition: Excuses performance when extraordinary events occur
force majeure.plain_english: Neither side is at fault if something unexpected happens (natural disaster, pandemic)

arbitration.definition: Dispute resolution where a private third party makes a binding decision
arbitration.plain_english: If there's a disagreement, you go to a private judge instead of court
arbitration.risk_note: May limit your legal rights — you usually can't appeal

governing law.definition: The jurisdiction whose laws will interpret and enforce the contract
governing law.plain_english: Which state/country's laws apply if there's a dispute
governing law.risk_note: Make sure it's a jurisdiction you can access

assignment.definition: Transfer of contractual rights or obligations to a third party
assignment.plain_english: Whether either party can hand off responsibilities to someone else
assignment.risk_note: Anti-assignment clauses protect you from dealing with an unknown party

confidentiality.definition: An obligation to keep certain information private
confidentiality.risk_note: Indefinite confidentiality obligations are very restrictive

intellectual property.definition: Creative works, inventions, and ideas with legal protections
intellectual property.plain_english: Who owns the ideas, code, or creative work produced under this contract
intellectual property.risk_note: IP assignment can transfer YOUR work to them

warranty.definition: A promise or guarantee that something is true or will happen
warranty.plain_english: A promise that the product/service will work as described

as is.definition: Provided without any warranties or guarantees
as is.plain_english: They're not promising it works — you get it in current condition
as is.risk_note: Very risky for buyers — no recourse if something is wrong

waiver.definition: Voluntarily giving up a legal right or claim
waiver.plain_english: You're agreeing to give up certain rights
waiver.risk_note: Read carefully what rights you're waiving

severability.definition: If one part of the contract is invalid, the rest still applies
severability.plain_english: If a court strikes down one clause, the rest still stands

liquidated damages.definition: Pre-determined compensation amount for a breach
liquidated damages.plain_english: If you break the contract, you owe a specific dollar amount decided upfront
liquidated damages.risk_note: Check if the amount is reasonable

auto-renewal.definition: Contract automatically extends unless one party opts out
auto-renewal.plain_english: This contract keeps renewing unless you actively cancel it
auto-renewal.risk_note: Check the notice period — you might need to cancel 30-90 days before renewal

sole discretion.definition: One party has absolute decision-making power
sole discretion.plain_english: They can decide whatever they want without explanation

perpetual.definition: Lasting forever, with no end date
perpetual.plain_english: This obligation or right never expires
perpetual.risk_note: Negotiate an end date if possible

irrevocable.definition: Cannot be taken back or undone once granted
irrevocable.plain_english: Once you agree, you can't change your mind — ever
irrevocable.risk_note: Be very careful with irrevocable rights

mutual.plain_english: This applies to both sides equally

unilateral.plain_english: Only ONE side has to follow this rule

breach.definition: A violation or failure to fulfill a contractual obligation
breach.plain_english: Breaking the rules of the contract
breach.risk_note: Understand what counts as breach and the penalties

material breach.definition: A significant violation undermining the core purpose of the contract
material breach.plain_english: A serious rule-break that defeats the whole point of the agreement

good faith.definition: An obligation to act honestly and fairly
good faith.plain_english: Both sides must be honest and fair — no sneaky tricks
good faith.risk_note: Implied in most contracts even if not stated

change of control.definition: Provisions applying when a party is acquired or ownership changes
change of control.plain_english: What happens to this contract if the company gets bought
change of control.risk_note: You could end up bound to a different company

right of first refusal.definition: The right to match any offer before the other party accepts elsewhere
right of first refusal.plain_english: They must give you the chance to match any competing offer first
